title: GLM-OCR
oneLiner: Open OCR model and pipeline for turning complex document images into usable text.
summary: GLM-OCR is an open OCR model and document pipeline from Z.ai, focused on accurate, fast, and comprehensive image-to-text extraction for documents, tables, formulas, and complex layouts.
whyItMatters: GLM-OCR matters because many real AI workflows begin with messy documents, not clean chat messages. A strong open OCR layer can become the front door for PDF analysis, retrieval systems, research workflows, and agent tools that need reliable document ingestion.
bestFor: Builders working on document AI, PDF processing, or knowledge ingestion
Teams that need an open OCR component before RAG or agent workflows
Researchers comparing modern OCR pipelines beyond generic vision-language models
notFor: Users who want a fully managed consumer product with no setup work
Teams that cannot review the linked source, license, and operational requirements before adoption
seoTitle: GLM-OCR: Models resource for open AI builders
seoDescription: GLM-OCR profile on OpenAgent.bot: what it is, when to use it, official links, open-source status, and structured resource data.
shareDescription: Open OCR model and pipeline for turning complex document images into usable text.

coreStrengths.title: Document-first model focus
coreStrengths.description: GLM-OCR targets OCR and image-to-text extraction rather than general chat.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Specialization is valuable when a workflow depends on layout, tables, equations, and structured document text.

coreStrengths.title: Open model and pipeline licensing
coreStrengths.description: The repository states MIT licensing for the model and Apache-2.0 licensing for code components.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Clear licensing makes it easier to evaluate for production document workflows.

coreStrengths.title: Useful for agent intake
coreStrengths.description: OCR output can feed downstream agents, search indexes, and retrieval systems.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Agents are only as useful as the documents and screens they can accurately read.

useCaseNotes.title: PDF and document ingestion
useCaseNotes.description: Convert scans and visual documents into text before indexing or summarization.

useCaseNotes.title: Research workflow automation
useCaseNotes.description: Extract usable text from papers, reports, forms, and tables for downstream analysis.

useCaseNotes.title: RAG preprocessing
useCaseNotes.description: Use OCR as the first stage before chunking, embedding, and retrieval.

compareNotes.title: Choose GLM-OCR for document pipelines
compareNotes.summary: A general multimodal model may describe an image, but GLM-OCR is the better starting point when the job is faithful document extraction.

commandLine.label: Clone GLM-OCR
commandLine.description: Use the official repository examples for the current vLLM or local inference setup.

faq.question: Is GLM-OCR open source?
faq.answer: GLM-OCR is listed with MIT model / Apache-2.0 code based on its official source links. Always re-check the repository or model card before production use.
